info.name: Gunslinger
info.description: Gunslinger from The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

element.name: Gunslinger
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: Risk is in a Gunslinger's blood. They are bold renegades, bucking tradition and forging a new path with dangerous and inelegant firearms. Gunslingers are infamous for surviving by their wits and relying on split-second timing and a considerable amount of luck to survive.

Black powder isn't for the faint of heart. Its thunderous applause is volatile and imprecise—a barely controlled explosion directed at an enemy. Only the truly fearless seek to master it. But Gunslingers have nerves of steel, hurling death from their guns in a roaring cacophony. Adapted for shootouts, gunslingers are mobile and daring, knowing that life or death hangs on snap decision-making and one's own mettle.

A Gunslinger's explosive lifestyle lends well to wandering and adventuring. Gunslingers often shoot first and ask questions later, an attitude which earns them few friends and bountiful enemies. In their travels, most gunslingers are secretive and take great lengths to go unnoticed, lest they be spotted by old foes with scores to settle.

tr.td: Choose 2: Acrobatics, Animal Handling, Athletics, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Perception, Persuasion, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth
Simple weapons and Martial Ranged weapons
Choose A or B: (A) Leather Armor, 2 Daggers, Revolver, 50 Bullets, Explorer's Pack, and 11 GP; or (B) 175 GP

ul.li: Gain all the traits in the Core Gunslinger Traits table.
Gain the Gunslinger’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Rogue Features table.
Gain the following traits from the Core Gunslinger Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency with Martial Ranged weapons, and training with Light armor.
Gain the Gunslinger’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Gunslinger Features table.

tr.td: Fighting Style, Quick Draw, Weapon Mastery

description.p: As a Gunslinger, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Gunslinger levels. These features are listed in the Gunslinger Features table.

element.name: Level 1: Fighting Style
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. If you choose a feat, such as Great Weapon Fighting, that requires you to hold a Melee weapon in one or two hands, you can use that feat with Ranged weapons.
Whenever you gain a Gunslinger level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different Fighting Style feat.

sheet.description: If you choose a feat that requires you to hold a Melee weapon in one or two hands, you can use that feat with Ranged weapons.

element.name: Level 1: Quick Draw
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You're adept at drawing and firing before others have time to react, granting you the following benefits.
Initiative. You have Advantage on Initiative rolls.
Double Draw. You can draw or stow two weapons that lack the Two-Handed property when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one.

sheet.description: You have Advantage on Initiative rolls. Additionally, you can draw or stow two weapons that lack the Two-Handed property when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one.

element.name: Level 1: Weapon Mastery
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Ranged weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.
When you reach certain Gunslinger levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more kinds of weapons, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Gunslinger Features table.

sheet.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of the weapons you previously chosen for a different one.

element.name: Level 2: Critical Shot
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: Your attack rolls with Ranged weapons can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.
At Gunslinger level 9, your attack rolls with Ranged weapons score a Critical Hit on a roll of 18-20. At Gunslinger level 17, they score a Critical Hit on a roll of 17-20.

sheet.description: Your attack rolls with Ranged weapons can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.
Your attack rolls with Ranged weapons can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 18-20 on the d20.
Your attack rolls with Ranged weapons can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 17-20 on the d20.

element.name: Level 2: Risk
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You can perform incredible feats of daring fueled by special dice called Risk Dice.

Risk Dice. You have four Risk Dice, which are d8s. A Risk Die is expended when you use it. You regain all expended Risk Dice when you finish a Short or Long Rest. Your Risk Die changes and more Risk Dice become available as shown on the Risk Dice column of the Gunslinger Features table.
Maneuvers. You can expend Risk Dice to perform maneuvers. Your maneuver options are detailed later in the class description.
Saving Throws. If a maneuver requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus.

sheet.description: You have {{tgcvss:risk:number}} Risk Dice, which are d{{tgcvss:risk:dice}}s. A Risk Die is expended when you use it. You can expend Risk Dice to perform maneuvers. (DC {{tgcvss:risk:dc}})

element.name: Bite the Bullet
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Risk Die to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the number rolled on the die plus your Gunslinger level.

sheet.description: You can gain THPs equal to the number rolled on the die plus {{level:gunslinger}}.

element.name: Blindfire
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You can take a Bonus Action and expend one Risk Die to gain Blindsight with a range of 30 feet until the end of the current turn.

sheet.description: You can gain Blindsight with a range of 30 ft until the end of the current turn.

element.name: Dodge Roll
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You can expend one Risk Die as a Bonus Action to move up to 15 feet and reload any Ranged weapon you are holding. This movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks and is unaffected by Difficult Terrain.

sheet.description: You can move up to 15 ft and reload any Ranged weapon you are holding. This movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks and is unaffected by Difficult Terrain.

element.name: Grazing Shot
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you miss with a ranged attack roll using a weapon, you can expend one Risk Die (no action required) to deal damage to that creature equal to a roll of the die plus your Dexterity modifier (minimum of 1). This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier. You can only use this maneuver once per turn.

sheet.description: When you miss with a ranged attack roll using a weapon, you can deal {{tgcvss:grazing shot:modifier}} damage to that creature. This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier.

element.name: Maverick Spirit
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma ability check or saving throw, you can expend one Risk Die to add it to the roll, potentially turning it into a success. You can only use this maneuver once per turn.

sheet.description: When you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma ability check or saving throw, you can expend one Risk Die to add it to the roll, potentially turning it into a success.

element.name: Skin of Your Teeth
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction and expend one Risk Die to dodge out of harm's way. Roll the die and add the number rolled to your AC against this attack, potentially causing it to miss.

sheet.description: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can dodge out of harm's way. Roll the die and add the number rolled to your AC against this attack, potentially causing it to miss.

element.name: Level 3: Gunslinger Subclass
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You gain a Gunslinger subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Gunslinger levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Gunslinger level or lower.

element.name: Level 4: Ability Score Improvement
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk levels 8, 12, and 16.

element.name: Level 5: Extra Attack
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

element.name: Level 5: Gut Shot
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: Whenever you score a Critical Hit against a Large or smaller creature with a ranged attack using a weapon, the projectile lodges itself in the target. For 1 minute or until the target replaces one of its attacks with dislodging the projectile, its Speed is halved and it has Disadvantage on attack rolls.

sheet.description: Whenever you score a Critical Hit against a Large or smaller creature with a ranged attack using a weapon, the projectile lodges itself in the target. For 1 minute or until the target replaces one of its attacks with dislodging the projectile, its Speed is halved and it has Disadvantage on attack rolls.

element.name: Level 7: Evasion
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail.
You don't benefit from this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

sheet.description: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You don't benefit from this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

element.name: Level 11: Overkill
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you deal damage with a Ranged weapon that doesn't add your ability modifier to the roll, you add your ability modifier nonetheless. If you already add your modifier to the damage roll, the target takes an extra 1d8 damage of the weapon's type.
Note that weapons that have the Firearm property don't add your ability modifier to damage rolls.

sheet.description: When you deal damage with a Ranged weapon that doesn't add your ability modifier to the roll, you add your ability modifier nonetheless. If you already add your modifier to the damage roll, the target takes an extra 1d8 damage of the weapon's type.

element.name: Level 13: Cheat Death
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points and not killed outright, you can drop to 1 Hit Point instead, and you regain a number of Hit Points equal to your Gunslinger level.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

sheet.description: When you are reduced to 0 HPs and not killed outright, you can drop to 1 HP instead, and you regain {{level:gunslinger}} HPs.

element.name: Level 15: Dire Gambit
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: Whenever you roll Initiative or score a Critical Hit, you regain one expended Risk Die.

sheet.description: Whenever you roll Initiative or score a Critical Hit, you regain one expended Risk Die.

element.name: Level 18: Deft Maneuver
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You gain a special additional Bonus Action that you can take once on each of your turns. You can take this special Bonus Action only to use a maneuver.

sheet.description: You gain a special additional Bonus Action. You can take this special Bonus Action only to use a maneuver.

element.name: Level 19: Epic Boon
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Irresistible Offense is recommended.

element.name: Level 20: Headshot
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you score a Critical Hit against a creature using a Ranged weapon, you can choose for it to be a Headshot. If the creature has less than 100 Hit Points, it dies. Otherwise, it takes an extra 10d10 damage of the weapon's type.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can also restore your use of it by expending three Risk Dice (no action required).

sheet.description: When you score a Critical Hit against a creature using a Ranged weapon, you can choose for it to be a Headshot. If the creature has less than 100 HPs, it dies. Otherwise, it takes an extra 10d10 damage of the weapon's type. You can also restore your use of this feature by expending 3 Risk Dice.

element.name: Maneuver Options
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: Bite the Bullet. As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Risk Die to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the number rolled on the die plus your Gunslinger level.
Blindfire. You can take a Bonus Action and expend one Risk Die to gain Blindsight with a range of 30 feet until the end of the current turn.

Dodge Roll. You can expend one Risk Die as a Bonus Action to move up to 15 feet and reload any Ranged weapon you are holding. This movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks and is unaffected by Difficult Terrain.

Grazing Shot. When you miss with a ranged attack roll using a weapon, you can expend one Risk Die (no action required) to deal damage to that creature equal to a roll of the die plus your Dexterity modifier (minimum of 1). This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier. You can only use this maneuver once per turn.

Maverick Spirit. When you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma ability check or saving throw, you can expend one Risk Die to add it to the roll, potentially turning it into a success. You can only use this maneuver once per turn.

Skin of Your Teeth. When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction and expend one Risk Die to dodge out of harm's way. Roll the die and add the number rolled to your AC against this attack, potentially causing it to miss.

element.name: Risk: Bite the Bullet Feature Replacement
Risk: Skin of Your Teeth Feature Replacement
